common.name: Name
common.noAccess: No accessible pages. Contact an administrator.

settings.title: Settings
settings.changePasswordDesc: Change your password via the Keycloak account management page

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.subtitle: Overview of database connections and health
dashboard.loadFailed: Failed to load health data. Is the Query Service running?
dashboard.noDatabasesDesc: Go to the Connections page to add your first database.

connections.title: Connections
connections.subtitle: Manage database connections
connections.noConnectionsDesc: Click "Add Connection" to register your first database.
connections.noSearchResultsDesc: Clear the search or try another alias, type, host, port, database, or status.
connections.deleteConfirm: Delete database "{{alias}}"? This action cannot be undone.
connections.assigneesHint: Emails notified of alerts (comma/newline separated, optional).

permissions.title: Permissions
permissions.subtitle: Configure role-based database access
permissions.noPermissionsDesc: Add a role-database permission using the form above.
permissions.noSearchResultsDesc: Clear the search or try another role, database, operation, or table.
permissions.cancelTableAccess: Cancel table access edit for {{role}} on {{db}}

auditLogs.title: Audit Logs
auditLogs.subtitle: Query execution history and audit trail
auditLogs.noLogsDesc: Adjust your filters or wait for queries to be executed.

adminAuditLogs.title: Admin Audit Log
adminAuditLogs.subtitle: Configuration change history for routes, upstreams, and API keys
adminAuditLogs.toggleDetails: Toggle details for admin audit log {{id}}
adminAuditLogs.noLogsDesc: Adjust your filters or wait for configuration changes to occur.

queryPlayground.title: Query Playground
queryPlayground.subtitle: Execute SQL queries against connected databases
queryPlayground.truncatedWarning: Results were truncated. Only the first {{count}} rows are shown.
queryPlayground.noRows: Query executed successfully. No rows returned.
queryPlayground.historyHint: Your recent query executions. Click a row to load it into the editor.
queryPlayground.historyEmptyDesc: Queries you execute will appear here.
queryPlayground.savedHint: Your saved queries. Load one into the editor or delete it.
queryPlayground.savedEmptyDesc: Use the Save Query button to keep frequently used queries.
queryPlayground.savedNoSearchResultsDesc: Clear the search or try another saved query name, database, description, or SQL fragment.

queryTemplates.title: Query Templates
queryTemplates.subtitle: Expose saved read-only database queries as stable API paths
queryTemplates.parameterSyntaxIntro: SQL placeholder syntax depends on the database. Match the name with the key in Params JSON.
queryTemplates.paramsJsonHelp: Params JSON is always an object. Use the same key name as the SQL placeholder variable.
queryTemplates.parameterSyntaxWarning: Do not pass SQL fragments such as table or column names through Params JSON.
queryTemplates.noTemplatesDesc: Create a template to expose a saved query path.
queryTemplates.noSearchResultsDesc: Clear the search or try another template name, path, database, or status.
queryTemplates.paramsMustBeObject: Params JSON must be an object

gatewayRoutes.title: Gateway Routes
gatewayRoutes.subtitle: Manage API gateway routing rules
gatewayRoutes.noRoutesDesc: Click "Add Route" to create your first API route.
gatewayRoutes.noSearchResultsDesc: Clear the search or try another route name, URI, method, or upstream.
gatewayRoutes.deleteConfirm: Delete route "{{name}}"? This cannot be undone.

gatewayRouteForm.newSubtitle: Create a new API gateway route
gatewayRouteForm.uriHint: Must start with /api/. Append /* to include all sub-paths
gatewayRouteForm.timeoutHint: Leave blank to use the global default timeout (set in Query Settings). Enter a value to override it for this route only.
gatewayRouteForm.serviceKeyDesc: Auth headers to auto-add when requesting upstream (e.g., external API Bearer token). Add as many as you need.
gatewayRouteForm.assigneesHint: Emails notified of this route's alerts (comma/newline separated, optional).

gatewayUpstreams.title: Gateway Upstreams
gatewayUpstreams.subtitle: Manage backend server groups
gatewayUpstreams.schemeHint: Protocol APISIX uses when connecting to this upstream
gatewayUpstreams.hostHeaderHint: External HTTPS upstreams usually need the upstream node host
gatewayUpstreams.typeHint: Load balancing algorithm for distributing traffic to nodes
gatewayUpstreams.nodesHint: List of backend servers to handle requests. Higher weight receives more traffic
gatewayUpstreams.noUpstreamsDesc: Click "Add Upstream" to register a backend server group.
gatewayUpstreams.noSearchResultsDesc: Clear the search or try another name, scheme, type, host, or node.

apiKeys.title: API Keys
apiKeys.subtitle: Create API keys and manage access to data sources and routes
apiKeys.writePermissionsHint: SELECT is always allowed. Check the write statements this key may execute; DDL is never allowed.
apiKeys.allowedTablesHint: Comma-separated table names. Leave empty to allow all tables.
apiKeys.descriptionPlaceholder: Describe what this key is for
apiKeys.keyCreatedMessage: API key created. Copy it now — you won't be able to see it again.
apiKeys.noKeysDesc: Click "Add API Key" to create your first API key.
apiKeys.noSearchResultsDesc: Clear the search or try another key name, description, owner, or access label.

myApiKey.title: My API Key
myApiKey.subtitle: Issue and manage your personal API key for the query and storage APIs
myApiKey.loadFailed: Failed to load your API key.
myApiKey.scopeSummary: All databases & S3 · LLM excluded · 30 requests/min
myApiKey.noKeyDesc: Create your personal API key to access the query and storage APIs.
myApiKey.keyCreatedMessage: Here is your API key. Copy it now — it is shown only once and cannot be retrieved again.
myApiKey.regenerateConfirm: Regenerate your API key? The current key will stop working immediately.
myApiKey.deleteConfirm: Delete your API key? This cannot be undone.
myApiKey.alreadyExists: You already have an API key.
myApiKey.expiredBanner: Your API key has expired. Renew it to keep using the same key.
myApiKey.renewDesc: Renewing extends the expiry by 30 days while keeping the same key value.
myApiKey.renewSuccess: API key renewed for 30 more days.

timeRange.label: Time range
timeRange.invalid: Start must be before end, span at least 60 seconds, and end cannot be in the future.

breakdown.selectBucketHint: Select Hourly, Daily or Weekly to see usage over time.

externalMonitoring.title: External API Monitoring
externalMonitoring.subtitle: Traffic stats for registered services that don't route through the gateway
externalMonitoring.loadFailed: Failed to load metrics. Is Prometheus running?
externalMonitoring.partialLoadFailed: Some metrics failed to load. Data may be incomplete.
externalMonitoring.noServiceData: No service traffic data available. Register services on the Servers page and expose the metric convention.
externalMonitoring.filteredNote: Filtered to '{{key}}'. Clear the service filter to compare all services.
externalMonitoring.noEndpointData: No endpoint data available. The service must label requests with a route pattern (handler).

metricsGuide.title: API Metrics Guide
metricsGuide.subtitle: How to expose traffic metrics for UniBridge monitoring without gateway onboarding

externalServices.title: External services
externalServices.subtitle: APIs monitored without gateway onboarding — scraped from their /metrics endpoint
externalServices.empty: No external services registered. Add one to collect its traffic stats.
externalServices.name: Service name
externalServices.conventionHint: The service must expose http_requests_total and http_request_duration_seconds — see docs/api-metrics-convention.md.

gatewayMonitoring.title: Gateway Monitoring
gatewayMonitoring.subtitle: Real-time API traffic and performance metrics
gatewayMonitoring.loadFailed: Failed to load metrics. Is Prometheus running?
gatewayMonitoring.partialLoadFailed: Some metrics failed to load. Data may be incomplete.
gatewayMonitoring.noApiKeyData: No API key traffic data available
gatewayMonitoring.selfScopeNote: Showing traffic for your API key only
gatewayMonitoring.filteredNote: Filtered to '{{key}}'. Clear the API key filter to compare all keys.

llmMonitoring.title: LLM Monitoring
llmMonitoring.subtitle: Token usage, cost tracking, and LLM request analytics
llmMonitoring.loadFailed: Failed to load LLM metrics. Is Prometheus running?
llmMonitoring.partialLoadFailed: Some LLM metrics failed to load. Data may be incomplete.
llmMonitoring.tokenChartCaption: Prompt + Completion stack to the total; the Cached bar is the portion of prompt tokens served from cache.
llmMonitoring.noKeyData: No API key usage data available
llmMonitoring.statusSourceCaption: Measured at the gateway (APISIX), including requests rejected before reaching LiteLLM — totals can exceed the LiteLLM-based request counts above.
llmMonitoring.filteredNote: Filtered to '{{key}}'. Clear the API key filter to compare all keys.

roles.title: Roles
roles.subtitle: Manage roles and their permissions
roles.noRolesDesc: Create a role to group permissions for users and API access.
roles.noSearchResultsDesc: Clear the search or try another role name, description, type, or permission.

users.title: Users
users.subtitle: Manage Keycloak users and roles
users.noUsersDesc: Create a user or wait for a Keycloak account to appear.
users.noSearchResultsDesc: Clear the search or try another username, email, role, or status.
users.temporary: Temporary (user must change on next login)

querySettings.title: Query Settings
querySettings.subtitle: Configure query execution limits and SQL restrictions
querySettings.rateLimitHint: Maximum number of query requests a single user can make per minute
querySettings.maxConcurrentHint: Maximum number of queries a single user can run simultaneously
querySettings.defaultRowLimitHint: Maximum rows returned when a query does not specify its own limit; excess rows are truncated
querySettings.queryRouteTimeoutHint: How long the APISIX gateway waits for a query response before cutting the connection. Applies live without a restart. Keep it above the query's own timeout (max 300s) so slow queries aren't dropped at the gateway
querySettings.gatewayRouteTimeoutHint: Default read/send timeout applied to registered gateway routes that don't set their own. Changing this re-applies live to all routes without a per-route override. Per-route values are set on each route's edit page
querySettings.blockedKeywordsHint: Comma-separated list of SQL keywords to block (e.g. VACUUM, ANALYZE). Built-in blocks: GRANT, REVOKE, SHUTDOWN, KILL, etc.

s3.title: S3 Connections
s3.subtitle: Manage S3-compatible storage connections
s3.endpointUrlHint: Endpoint for S3-compatible storage (MinIO, R2, etc.). Leave blank for AWS S3
s3.defaultBucketHint: Bucket to open by default in browser (optional)
s3.noConnectionsDesc: Click "Add S3 Connection" to register your first S3 storage.
s3.noSearchResultsDesc: Clear the search or try another alias, endpoint, region, bucket, or status.
s3.proxyDownloadHint: Direct download via UniBridge, up to 500MB
s3.presignedUrlHint: Get signed URL for direct S3 access (not reachable from browser for internal S3)

nas.title: NAS Connections
nas.subtitle: Manage read-only NAS / local-filesystem connections
nas.basePathHint: Absolute path on the server's mounted filesystem (must be under an allowed root)
nas.maxDownloadMbHint: Per-connection download cap in bytes (optional; may only lower the global ceiling)
nas.maxDownloadBytesHint: Per-connection download cap in bytes (optional; may only lower the global ceiling)
nas.showHiddenHint: Include dotfiles and OS junk in listings
nas.followSymlinksHint: Allow following symbolic links within the base path
nas.noConnectionsDesc: Click "Add NAS Connection" to register your first NAS storage.
nas.noSearchResultsDesc: Clear the search or try another alias, base path, option, or status.
nas.proxyDownloadHint: Direct download via UniBridge, up to 500MB
nas.previewTooLarge: File is too large to preview. Download it instead.
nas.previewTruncated: File is large — showing a portion only. Download for the full content.
nas.truncatedNotice: This directory has too many entries to list fully — showing a partial set. Narrow it with a search term.

alerts.settingsSubtitle: Manage assignees, admins, and the delivery channel.
alerts.adminEmailsHint: Emails that receive every alert. Separate with commas or new lines.
alerts.assigneesHint: Assignee emails notified of this resource's alerts. Separate with commas or new lines.
alerts.recipientItemTemplateHelp: Used to render each owner email into a mail recipient object. Include {{email}}; payload templates can use {{recipients_json}}.
alerts.routeErrorMinRequestsHelp: A route must receive at least this many requests over the 5-minute window before its error rate can trigger an alert. Suppresses noisy alerts on low-traffic routes. 0 disables.
alerts.triggerAfterFailuresHelp: An alert fires only after this many consecutive failed checks (1–10).
alerts.recipientsDuplicateHint: For channels that accept only one recipient per call (e.g. mail APIs), register the same channel multiple times with one recipient per row.
alerts.statusSubtitle: Current health of monitored targets. Refreshes every 15 seconds.
alerts.statusNoneAlerting: No targets are alerting right now.
alerts.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete?
alerts.templateHelp: The following variables can be used in the payload template.
alerts.varDesc_alert_type: Alert type (db_health, nas_health, upstream_health, error_rate, route_error_rate)
alerts.varDesc_recipients_json: Rendered JSON array of mail recipient items
alerts.varDesc_rate: Current error rate (used in error-rate-based alerts)
alerts.varDesc_threshold: Threshold value configured in the rule

pending.title: Awaiting approval
pending.message: Your registration was received. An administrator must approve your account before you can use the service.

servers.title: Servers
servers.subtitle: Monitor host CPU, memory, and disk via node_exporter, with proactive alerts.
servers.empty: No servers registered yet. Add one running node_exporter to start monitoring.
servers.name: Name
servers.addressHint: host:port of the node_exporter agent (default port 39100).
servers.diskMountpointsHint: Comma-separated node_exporter mountpoints. Leave blank to inherit the global default.
servers.noSearchResultsDesc: Clear the search or try another name, address, mountpoint, description, or status.
servers.thresholdsHint: Leave blank to inherit the global defaults from Alert settings.
servers.noMetricData: No metric data in this window.
